PLAINTIFF SEEKS PERMISSION PER CCP 405.36 TO RECORD A LIS PENDENS AFTER EXPUNGEMENT. BOTH TIMES THE COURT EXPUNGED THE LIS PENDENS/ERRATA ON PROCEDURAL GROUNDS (PER CCP 405.20 AND FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 405.36) AND STAYED THIS PARTITION ACTION WHICH CANNOT PROCEED IN THE ABSENCE OF A VALID LIS PENDENS. PLAINTIFF NOW MOVES TO RECORD A LIS PENDENS AS REQUIRED BY CCP 872.250. THE NATURE OF THE CASE IS DESCRIBED IN THE MOVING PAPERS, CCP 872.250 IS REFERENCED IN THE MOVING PAPERS, RELEVANT DOCUMENTS, INCLUDING ORDERS, ARE ATTACHED TO THE MOVING PAPERS. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF'S LACK OF ATTENTION TO DETAIL CREATES AMBIGUITY AND URGES PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO PROOFREAD PAPERS INTENDED FOR FILING/RECORDATION. SEE DISCREPANCY IN THE HEARING DATE ON THE NOTICE AND DECLARATION; REFERENCE TO A WRONG ACTION WITHIN OCTOBER 14, 2020 FILING. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT ITS DECISION IS NOT BASED ON THE SECTIONS OF THE REPLY THAT WERE THE SUBJECT TO DEFENDANT'S MARCH 21, 2022 EX-PARTE APPLICATION. Off Calendar Mar-23-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Kevin C. Menes; 209-938-7285; appearing on behalf of defendant and cross-complainant Jean I. Menes.
Stanley Riddell; Law Offices of Stanley Riddell; 415-787-0576; appearing on behalf of plaintiff Donald A. Peterson.

Defendant's counsel appears to contest the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's counsel objects to the timeliness of defendant's notice of appearance. Defendant's counsel admits that notice of their appearance was not timely provided to opposing counsel. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DONALD PETERSON'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO FILE AND RECORD SUPERSEDING LIS PENDENS is GRANTED. Lis Pendens in the proper form with notice provided to all entitled to such notice shall be recorded, filed and served forthwith. Plaintiff seeks permission per CCP 405.36 to record a lis pendens after expungement. Both times the Court expunged the Lis Pendens/Errata on procedural grounds (per CCP 405.20 and for failure to comply with CCP 405.36) and stayed this partition action which cannot proceed in the absence of a valid lis pendens. Plaintiff now moves to record a lis pendens as required by CCP 872.250. The nature of the case is described in the moving papers, CCP 872.250 is referenced in the moving papers, relevant documents, including orders, are attached to the moving papers. Defendant's reliance on factually and procedurally distinguishable Ranchito Ownership v. Sup. Ct. (1982) 130 Cal.App.3d 764 is misplaced. The Court notes that Plaintiff's lack of attention to detail creates ambiguity and urges Plaintiff's counsel to proofread papers intended for filing/recordation. See discrepancy in the hearing date on the Notice and Declaration; reference to a wrong action within October 14, 2020 filing. The Court further notes that its decision is not based on the sections of the reply that were the subject to Defendant's March 21, 2022 ex-parte application.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
